name: Palenquero (Lengua ri Palenge)

ancestors.notes: Spanish is the lexifier; the great majority of the lexicon and the seseo/yeismo phonology derive from (Caribbean/Andalusian) Spanish.

Kikongo (Bantu) substrate: the founders of San Basilio de Palenque were Kikongo/Kimbundu-speaking maroons. The most-cited substrate diagnostic is the word-initial prenasalized stops [mb nd ŋɡ] (ndulo < duro, ngota < gota, mbala < bala), categorically absent from the local Spanish of the same community (APiCS survey 48; Schwegler).

sources.author: Schwegler, Armin (APiCS survey chapter)
sources.title: Palenquero structure dataset (Survey chapter 48)
sources.publisher: Atlas of Pidgin and Creole Language Structures Online (APiCS), eds. Michaelis, Maurer, Haspelmath & Huber
sources.pages: Vowel inventory /i e a o u/ (Caribbean-Spanish-type); consonant inventory /p t k b d ɡ ʝ f m n ɲ tʃ s x l ɾ r/; word-initial prenasalized stops [mb nd ŋɡ] as African-substrate diagnostic (ngota, ndulo, mbala); seseo (no /θ/) and yeismo (/ʝ/, no /ʎ/) inferable from the inventory; 'one phoneme / one letter' modern orthography (Palenke, kumé).
sources.notes: Primary source for the phonemic inventory, the prenasalized-stop diagnostic and the phonemic school orthography.

sources.author: Lipski, John M.
sources.title: The 'new' Palenquero: revitalization and re-creolization
sources.publisher: In: Colombian Varieties of Spanish, eds. File-Muriel & Orozco, Iberoamericana/Vervuert, pp. 21-42
sources.pages: pp. 24-36: genuine glossed example sentences; TAM particles ta (imperfective), taba/-ba (past/imperfect), a (perfective/completive), asé (habitual); pronouns i (1sg), bo (2sg), ele (3sg), suto (1pl), ané (3pl), enú (2pl); postposed negation nu; preposed plural marker ma; copula jue; relativizer lo ke; the etymological c/qu transcription used for the gold register.
sources.notes: Open-access primary source; supplies the verified example sentences used in the gold set and the TAM/pronoun/negation morphosyntax.

sources.author: Schwegler, Armin & Morton, Thomas
sources.title: Vernacular Spanish in a microcosm: Kateyano in El Palenque de San Basilio (Colombia)
sources.publisher: Revista Internacional de Lingüística Iberoamericana 1(1), pp. 97-159
sources.pages: Contrast between Palenquero (creole) and local Kateyano (village Spanish); the prenasalized stops occur in the creole but not the local Spanish; intervocalic /b d ɡ/ lenition to [β ð ɣ]; acute accent marks stressed/high-tone syllables.
sources.notes: Key Schwegler phonology reference (consulted via citation; no open-access PDF located). Cited for the prenasalization contrast and the stress/tone orthography; not read first-hand — see PR body.

notes: Palenquero (Lengua ri Palenge), the Spanish-lexifier creole of San Basilio de Palenque, Bolívar, Colombia (~3,500 speakers; the only surviving Spanish creole of Latin America). Kikongo (Bantu) substrate. KEY FEATURES: (1) FIVE-VOWEL SYSTEM /a e i o u/, Caribbean-Spanish-type. (2) WORD-INITIAL PRENASALIZED STOPS: ⟨mb nd ng⟩ = [mb nd ŋɡ] (ndá < dar, ndo < dos, ngombe 'cattle') — the chief substrate diagnostic, absent from the community's local Spanish. (3) SESEO: ⟨c⟩ before front vowels and ⟨z⟩ = [s], no /θ/. (4) YEISMO: ⟨ll⟩ = [j], no /ʎ/. (5) ⟨j⟩ and ⟨g⟩ before front vowels = [h] (aspiration; jende < gente, jambre < hambre, jue < fue). (6) BETACISM: ⟨v⟩ = [b]. (7) Acute accent marks stress/high tone, mapped here to the base vowel quality (no lexical-stress/tone model). MORPHOSYNTAX (Lipski 2012): preverbal TAM ta/taba/a/asé; pronouns i, bo, ele, suto, enú, ané; postposed negation nu; preposed plural ma. The gold register uses Lipski's (2012) etymological c/qu transcription; the spec also parses the k-based phonemic school orthography (Palenke, kumé). OPEN / UNVERIFIED (flagged, need Schwegler primary literature): final /n/ → [ŋ]; full /l/~/ɾ/~/d/ neutralization; coda-/s/ behaviour in the creole proper (vs. local Spanish); the exact spelling of /ɲ/. These are deliberately NOT modelled beyond the Spanish-inherited defaults.

location.notes: San Basilio de Palenque, Mahates municipality, Bolívar Department, Colombia.
